verses.text: Of the holy faith of Jesus Christ.

verses.text: Of prayers spoken on the ordination of bishops, and of the order of the Missa.

verses.text: Of the ordination of presbyters.

verses.text: Of the ordination of deacons.

verses.text: Of those who suffer persecution for the faith.

verses.text: Of the election of reader and sub-deacon.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the gift of healing.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the presbyter who abides in a place inconvenient for his office.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of those who are converted to the Christian religion.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of him who makes idols.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Various pursuits are enumerated, the followers of which are not to be admitted to the Christian religion until repentance is exhibited.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the place which the highest kings or princes shall occupy in the temple.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: That it is not meet for Christians to bear arms.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of works which are unlawful to Christians.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the Christian who marries a slave-woman.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the free woman.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the midwife; and that the women ought to be separate from the men in prayer.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the catechumen who suffers martyrdom before baptism.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the fast of the fourth and sixth holiday; and of Lent.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: That presbyters should assemble daily with the people in church.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the week of the Jews’ passover; and of him who knows not passover (Easter).

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: That every one be held to learn doctrine.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the care of the bishop over the sick.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of him on whom the care of the sick is enjoined; and of the time at which prayers are to be made.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the time at which exhortations are to be heard.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of him who frequents the temple every day.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: That the faithful ought to eat nothing before the holy communion.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: That care is to be well taken that nothing fall from the chalice to the ground.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: That a deacon may dispense the Eucharist to the people with permission of a bishop or presbyter.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: That widows and virgins ought to pray constantly.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: That commemoration should be made of the faithful dead every day, with the exception of the Lord’s day.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the sober behaviour of the secular in church.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: That deacons may pronounce the benediction and thanksgiving at the love-feasts when a bishop is not present.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: Of the first-fruits of the earth, and of vows.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: When a bishop celebrates the holy communion (Synaxis), the presbyters who stand by him should be clothed in white.

verses.book: Appendix to the Works of Hippolytus. Containing Dubious and Spurious Pieces.
verses.text: That no one ought to sleep on the night of the resurrection of our Lord Jesus Christ.
